sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presented to the office with a complaint of right knee pain after an injury at a trampoline park.
He reports hearing a snap and experiencing instant pain when landing, which has persisted for a week.
He has not taken any significant measures to manage the pain, aside from taking Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He denies any significant exercise regimen, but enjoys recreational activities like going to the trampoline park.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which he takes lisinopril 20mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
He reports that his blood sugars have been a bit better, but does not have a consistent tracking system.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and diabetes, for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
He has not had any recent blood pressure measurements, but his last recorded blood pressure was 180/80.
He has not obtained a blood pressure cuff as previously discussed.
Review of systems: The patient reports that his knee pain is localized to the lateral aspect, with no pain on the medial side.
He has a slight limp, but can bear weight and walk.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80, although the patient does not have a regular measurement system in place.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee, with a slight limp and decreased range of motion due to pain.
The heart rate and rhythm are normal, with no murmurs.
The lungs are clear bilaterally.
Test Results:
Right knee x-ray: No evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; diabetes; suspected blood pressure regulation issue.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for the right knee pain and referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20mg daily for hypertension and order an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1C and lipid panel to evaluate the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will return in one week for a follow-up appointment to review the results of the tests and adjust the treatment plan as necessary.
